purpose: Publish bounded entry paths for the main public audiences so reviewers, integrators, contributors, tool-builders, and maintainers can follow the right surfaces without guessing from folder layout alone.

audiences.summary: Use this path when evaluating maturity, public scope, strongest evidence, and review discipline.
completion_signals: can explain what the repo publishes and what it explicitly does not publish
can identify the strongest reviewer-facing machine-readable evidence
can describe the bounded limitations and review posture without reading the private runtime repo
bounded_non_goals: does not expose the full private N1Hub runtime
does not claim a hosted service SLA

audiences.summary: Use this path when wiring a parser, validator client, or consumer workflow against the published HTTP and schema surfaces.
completion_signals: can build requests and interpret bounded response envelopes
can identify the negative-path and graph-aware examples
can explain where the live validator remains stronger than public prose
can consume the projection layer through package exports from a fresh install, including TypeScript type resolution, without relying on repo-relative imports
bounded_non_goals: does not promise a public hosted endpoint
does not replace live validator behavior for edge-case semantics

audiences.summary: Use this path when preparing a docs, example, schema, or public-boundary change to the public specs surface.
completion_signals: can distinguish issue-first changes from pull-request-safe projection edits
can explain which reviewer and release surfaces must move together
can name the verification evidence expected for a release-facing change
bounded_non_goals: does not grant access to private upstream workflow internals
does not replace the stronger source-of-truth capsule and validator surfaces

audiences.summary: Use this path when generating tooling from public schemas, OpenAPI, and machine-readable contract summaries.
completion_signals: can locate stable machine-readable artifacts quickly
can map examples to contract surfaces without guessing
can explain the stronger-source hierarchy for public generation targets
can consume the projection layer through package exports from a fresh install, including TypeScript type resolution, without relying on repo-relative imports
bounded_non_goals: does not guarantee a feature-complete SDK
does not expose private runtime internals beyond the published contract surface

audiences.summary: Use this path when keeping reviewer, release, and audience-facing surfaces synchronized as the public repo evolves.
completion_signals: can explain which public role paths must be refreshed when the repo grows
can identify which summary layers must move with release evidence
can keep reviewer shortcuts aligned with the real public artifact set
bounded_non_goals: does not expose private operator playbooks
does not replace stronger release, provenance, and boundary evidence surfaces
